subject: Product Management
title: When Not to Experiment
description: A 60-second lesson on When Not to Experiment.

hook.text: Should you A/B test whether to comply with privacy regulations? Obviously not. Not every decision belongs in a test.

buildup.text: Some decisions are ethical, legal, or brand-related. Testing whether to add accessibility features implies it's optional.

discovery.text: Strategic bets — like entering a new market — can't be A/B tested. They require conviction, vision, and willingness to commit.

twist.text: Over-testing can paralyze a team. If every tiny decision requires an experiment, velocity drops and innovation stalls.

climax.text: The rule: experiment on how to execute a decision, not on whether to make it. Test the implementation, not the principle.

punchline.text: Test how, not whether. Some things are decided by values, not data.

quiz.question: When should teams avoid A/B testing?
options: For ethical, legal, brand, or strategic decisions — test the implementation, not the principle
Teams should always A/B test every decision
Only when they lack testing tools
